  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "250:                         continue;\n251:                     }\n252:                     emitWindowContents(window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n253:                 }\n254: \n255:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n256:                     evictingWindowState.clear();\n257:                 }\n258:                 registerCleanupTimer(window);\n259:             }\n260:         }\n261: \n262:         // side output input event if\n263:         // element not handled by any window\n264:         // late arriving tag has been set\n265:         // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n266:         // element timestamp\n267:         if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n268:             if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n269:                 sideOutput(element);\n270:             } else {\n271:                 this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n272:             }\n273:         }\n274:     }\n275: \n276:     @Override\n277:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n278: \n279:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n280:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n281:         evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n282:         evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n283: \n284:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n285: \n286:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n287:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n288:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n289:             if (stateWindow == null) {\n290:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n291:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. 20. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_keyword_search @ -
Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc$ session window late allow sideOutput"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1200: \\n1201: By default, late elements are dropped when the watermark is past the end of the window. However,\\n1202: Flink allows to specify a maximum *allowed lateness* for window operators. Allowed lateness\\n1203: specifies by how much time elements can be late before they are dropped, and its default value is 0.\\n1204: Elements that arrive after the watermark has passed the end of the window but before it passes the end of\\n1205: the window plus the allowed lateness, are still added to the window. Depending on the trigger used,\\n1206: a late but not dropped element may cause the window to fire again. This is the case for the `EventTimeTrigger`.\\n1207: \\n1208: In order to make this work, Flink keeps the state of windows until their allowed lateness expires. Once this happens, Flink removes the window and deletes its state, as\\n1209: also described in the [Window Lifecycle](#window-lifecycle) section.\\n1210: \\n1211: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":1215,\"startLine\":1200},{\"content\":\"314: \\n315: ### Session Windows\\n316: \\n317: The *session windows* assigner groups elements by sessions of activity. Session windows do not overlap and\\n318: do not have a fixed start and end time, in contrast to *tumbling windows* and *sliding windows*. Instead a\\n319: session window closes when it does not receive elements for a certain period of time, *i.e.*, when a gap of\\n320: inactivity occurred. A session window assigner can be configured with either a static *session gap* or with a \\n321: *session gap extractor* function which defines how long the period of inactivity is. When this period expires, \\n322: the current session closes and subsequent elements are assigned to a new session window.\\n323: \\n324: {{< img src=\\\"/fig/session-windows.svg\\\" alt=\\\"session windows\\\" >}} \\n325: \\n326: The following code snippets show how to use session windows.\\n327: \\n328: \",\"endLine\":328,\"startLine\":314},{\"content\":\"1193: \\n1194: ## Allowed Lateness\\n1195: \\n1196: When working with *event-time* windowing, it can happen that elements arrive late, *i.e.* the watermark that Flink uses to\\n1197: keep track of the progress of event-time is already past the end timestamp of a window to which an element belongs. See\\n1198: [event time]({{< ref \\\"docs/dev/datastream/event-time/generating_watermarks\\\" >}}) and especially [late elements]({{< ref \\\"docs/dev/datastream/event-time/generating_watermarks\\\" >}}#late-elements) for a more thorough\\n1199: discussion of how Flink deals with event time.\\n1200: \",\"endLine\":1200,\"startLine\":1193}],\"file\":\"docs/content/docs/dev/datastream/operators/windows.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc/-/blob/docs/content/docs/dev/datastream/operators/windows.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1161: \\n1162: ## Allowed Lateness\\n1163: \\n1164: \",\"endLine\":1164,\"startLine\":1161},{\"content\":\"299: \\n300: ### \u4f1a\u8bdd\u7a97\u53e3\uff08Session Windows\uff09\\n301: \\n302: \",\"endLine\":302,\"startLine\":299},{\"content\":\"308: \\n309: {{< img src=\\\"/fig/session-windows.svg\\\" alt=\\\"session windows\\\" >}} \\n310: \\n311: \",\"endLine\":311,\"startLine\":308}],\"file\":\"docs/content.zh/docs/dev/datastream/operators/windows.md\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc/-/blob/docs/content.zh/docs/dev/datastream/operators/windows.md\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"471:     /**\\n472:      * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\\n473:      * the given window.\\n474:      */\\n475:     protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\\n476:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\\n477:                 && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\\n478:     }\\n479: \",\"endLine\":479,\"startLine\":471},{\"content\":\"136:      */\\n137:     protected final long allowedLateness;\\n138: \\n139: \",\"endLine\":139,\"startLine\":136},{\"content\":\"533:      *\\n534:      * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\\n535:      */\\n536:     private long cleanupTime(W window) {\\n537:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n538:             long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\\n539:             return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n540:         } else {\\n541:             return window.maxTimestamp();\\n542:         }\\n543: \",\"endLine\":543,\"startLine\":533}],\"file\":\"flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/asyncprocessing/operators/windowing/AsyncWindowOperator.java\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/asyncprocessing/operators/windowing/AsyncWindowOperator.java\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"345:     .keyBy(...)\\n346:     .window(...)\\n347:     .sideOutputLateData(lateTag)\\n348:     .process(...);\\n349: \",\"endLine\":349,\"startLine\":345},{\"content\":\"192: \\n193: ### Window Assigners\\n194: \\n195: Flink has several built-in types of window assigners, which are illustrated below:\\n196: \\n197: {{< img src=\\\"/fig/window-assigners.svg\\\" alt=\\\"Window assigners\\\" width=\\\"80%\\\" >}}\\n198: \\n199: Some examples of what these window assigners might be used for, and how to specify them:\\n200: \\n201: * Tumbling time windows\\n202:   * _page views per minute_\\n203:   * `TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMinutes(1))`\\n204: * Sliding time windows\\n205:   * _page views per minute computed every 10 seconds_\\n206:   * `SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMinutes(1), Duration.ofSeconds(10))`\\n207: * Session windows \\n208:   * _page views per session, where sessions are defined by a gap of at least 30 minutes between sessions_\\n209:   * `EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofMinutes(30))`\\n210: \\n211: \",\"endLine\":211,\"startLine\":192},{\"content\":\"349:   \\n350: DataStream<Event> lateStream = result.getSideOutput(lateTag);\\n351: ```\\n352:             "content": "1200: \n1201: By default, late elements are dropped when the watermark is past the end of the window. However,\n1202: Flink allows to specify a maximum *allowed lateness* for window operators. Allowed lateness\n1203: specifies by how much time elements can be late before they are dropped, and its default value is 0.\n1204: Elements that arrive after the watermark has passed the end of the window but before it passes the end of\n1205: the window plus the allowed lateness, are still added to the window. Depending on the trigger used,\n1206: a late but not dropped element may cause the window to fire again. This is the case for the `EventTimeTrigger`.\n1207: \n1208: In order to make this work, Flink keeps the state of windows until their allowed lateness expires. Once this happens, Flink removes the window and deletes its state, as\n1209: also described in the [Window Lifecycle](#window-lifecycle) section.\n1210: \n1211: ... [chunk truncated]",

            "content": "314: \n315: ### Session Windows\n316: \n317: The *session windows* assigner groups elements by sessions of activity. Session windows do not overlap and\n318: do not have a fixed start and end time, in contrast to *tumbling windows* and *sliding windows*. Instead a\n319: session window closes when it does not receive elements for a certain period of time, *i.e.*, when a gap of\n320: inactivity occurred. A session window assigner can be configured with either a static *session gap* or with a \n321: *session gap extractor* function which defines how long the period of inactivity is. When this period expires, \n322: the current session closes and subsequent elements are assigned to a new session window.\n323: \n324: {{< img src=\"/fig/session-windows.svg\" alt=\"session windows\" >}} \n325: \n326: The following code snippets show how to use session windows.\n327: \n328: ",

See\n1198: [event time]({{< ref \"docs/dev/datastream/event-time/generating_watermarks\" >}}) and especially [late elements]({{< ref \"docs/dev/datastream/event-time/generating_watermarks\" >}}#late-elements) for a more thorough\n1199: discussion of how Flink deals with event time.\n1200: ",

        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc/-/blob/docs/content.zh/docs/dev/datastream/operators/windows.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "471:     /**\n472:      * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n473:      * the given window.\n474:      */\n475:     protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n476:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n477:                 && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n478:     }\n479: ",
            "endLine": 479,
            "startLine": 471
          },
          {
            "content": "136:      */\n137:     protected final long allowedLateness;\n138: \n139: ",
            "endLine": 139,
            "startLine": 136
          },
          {
            "content": "533:      *\n534:      * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n535:      */\n536:     private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n537:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n538:             long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n539:             return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n540:         } else {\n541:             return window.maxTimestamp();\n542:         }\n543: ",
            "endLine": 543,
            "startLine": 533
          }
        ],
        "file": "flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/asyncprocessing/operators/windowing/AsyncWindowOperator.java",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/runtime/asyncprocessing/operators/windowing/AsyncWindowOperator.java"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "345:     .keyBy(...)\n346:     .window(...)\n347:     .sideOutputLateData(lateTag)\n348:     .process(...);\n349: ",
            "endLine": 349,
            "startLine": 345
          },
          {
            "content": "192: \n193: ### Window Assigners\n194: \n195: Flink has several built-in types of window assigners, which are illustrated below:\n196: \n197: {{< img src=\"/fig/window-assigners.svg\" alt=\"Window assigners\" width=\"80%\" >}}\n198: \n199: Some examples of what these window assigners might be used for, and how to specify them:\n200: \n201: * Tumbling time windows\n202:   * _page views per minute_\n203:   * `TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMinutes(1))`\n204: * Sliding time windows\n205:   * _page views per minute computed every 10 seconds_\n206:   * `SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMinutes(1), Duration.ofSeconds(10))`\n207: * Session windows \n208:   * _page views per session, where sessions are defined by a gap of at least 30 minutes between sessions_\n209:   * `EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofMinutes(30))`\n210: \n211: ",
            "endLine": 211,
            "startLine": 192
          },
          {
            "content": "349:   \n350: DataStream<Event> lateStream = result.getSideOutput(lateTag);\n351: ```\n352: ",
            "endLine": 352,
            "startLine": 349
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/content/docs/learn-flink/streaming_analytics.md",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc/-/blob/docs/content/docs/learn-flink/streaming_analytics.md"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "172:             // side output input event if\n173:             // element not handled by any window\n174:             // late arriving tag has been set\n175:             // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n176:             // element timestamp\n177:             if (windowFutures.isEmpty() && isElementLate(element)) {\n178:                 if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n179:                     sideOutput(element);\n180:                 } else {\n181:                     this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n182:                 }\n183: ",
            "endLine": 183,
            "startLine": 172

    "content": "    @Test\n    void testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 0;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be dropped because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is sideoutput as late, reuse last timestamp\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testDropDueToLatenessSessionWithLatenessPurgingTrigger() throws Exception {\n\n        // this has the same output as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness() because\n        // the allowed lateness is too small to make a difference\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>",
    "filePath": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
    "numLines": 120,
    "startLine": 2570,
    "totalLines": 3975
  },

38. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug\n\n## Problem Statement\nFix a bug where late-arriving data elements for session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped.\n\n## Key Files\n- **Main Code**: `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` (lines 293-447, processElement)\n- **Test Code**: `flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n- **Related**: EvictingWindowOperator.java (extends WindowOperator, has same issue)\n\n## Current Status\n- Existing test `testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness` PASSES\n- All 52 WindowOperatorTest tests PASS\n- No failing tests found yet\n\n## Code Flow Analysis\n1. For merging windows (lines 303-403):\n   - Element assigned to windows via windowAssigner\n   - For each window: call `addWindow()` which merges with existing windows\n   - Check `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` - if true, retire window and skip\n   - If not late, process element normally\n   - At end: if all windows skipped AND `isElementLate()`, send to side output\n\n2. Late data side output (lines 440-446):\n   - Only done if `isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element) && lateDataOutputTag != null`\n\n## Potential Bug\nThe bug description mentions checking lateness BEFORE merging, but code shows merge happens first. Need to understand:\n1. When would a late element get silently dropped instead of side-outputted?\n2. What specific scenario is broken?\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Run existing test to confirm it passes\n2. Create new test case that reproduces the bug\n3. Identify root cause\n4. Implement fix\n5. Write comprehensive test\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug\n\n## Problem Statement\nFix a bug where late-arriving data elements for session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped.\n\n## Key Files\n- **Main Code**: `flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java` (lines 293-447, processElement)\n- **Test Code**: `flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n- **Related**: EvictingWindowOperator.java (extends WindowOperator, has same issue)\n\n## Current Status\n- Existing test `testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionZeroLateness` PASSES\n- All 52 WindowOperatorTest tests PASS\n- No failing tests found yet\n\n## Code Flow Analysis\n1. For merging windows (lines 303-403):\n   - Element assigned to windows via windowAssigner\n   - For each window: call `addWindow()` which merges with existing windows\n   - Check `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` - if true, retire window and skip\n   - If not late, process element normally\n   - At end: if all windows skipped AND `isElementLate()`, send to side output\n\n2. Late data side output (lines 440-446):\n   - Only done if `isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element) && lateDataOutputTag != null`\n\n## Potential Bug\nThe bug description mentions checking lateness BEFORE merging, but code shows merge happens first. Need to understand:\n1. When would a late element get silently dropped instead of side-outputted?\n2. What specific scenario is broken?\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Run existing test to confirm it passes\n2. Create new test case that reproduces the bug\n3. Identify root cause\n4. Implement fix\n5. Write comprehensive test\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}
39. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "import java.time.Duration;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.BasicTypeInfo;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeInformation;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple3;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.EventTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.EventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.TimeWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.WindowOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.WindowOperatorTestBase;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.watermark.Watermark;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\n/**\n * Test to demonstrate late data side output handling for merging windows.\n *\n * This test exercises the scenario where:\n * 1. An element arrives late (after watermark)\n * 2. Could potentially merge with an existing non-expired window\n * 3. Should be emitted to side output\n */\npublic class TestSessionWindowLateData {\n\n    private final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late\"){};\n\n    @Test\n    void testLateElementInMergingSessionWindow() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 5;  // 5 second gap\n        final long allowedLateness = 0;  // No allowed lateness\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        (a, b) -> new Tuple2<>(a.f0, a.f1 + b.f1),\n                        TypeInformation.of(new org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils.GenericTypeInfo<>(\n                                Tuple2.class,\n                                String.class,\n                                Integer.class\n                        )));\n\n        WindowOperator<String, Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, TimeWindow> operator =\n                new WindowOperator<>(\n                        EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                        new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                        new org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public String getKey(Tuple2<String, Integer> value) {\n                                return value.f0;\n                            }\n                        },\n                        BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                new org.apache.flink.api.common.ExecutionConfig().getSerializerConfig()),\n                        stateDesc,\n                        new InternalWindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                                String, TimeWindow>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public void process(String key, TimeWindow window,\n                                    InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext context,\n                                    Tuple2<String, Integer> input,\n                                    org.apache.flink.util.Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out) {\n                                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n                            }\n\n                            @Override\n                            public void clear(TimeWindow window,\n                                    InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext context) {\n                            }\n                        },\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        allowedLateness,\n                        lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                harness = new OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<>(operator);\n\n        harness.open();\n\n        // Scenario:\n        // 1. Element arrives at timestamp 1000, creates window [1000, 1005]\n        // 2. Watermark advances to 1010 (window is now \"late\" - cleanup time is 1005)\n        // 3. Another element arrives at timestamp 1003 (which is LATE - 1003 + 0 <= 1010)\n        // 4. This element COULD merge with the first window, creating [1000, 1008]\n        // 5. But the merged window is ALSO late (1008 + 0 <= 1010)\n        // 6. The element should be sent to side output, but might be silently dropped\n\n        System.out.println(\"Step 1: Process element at ts=1000\");\n        harness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1000L));\n\n        System.out.println(\"Step 2: Advance watermark to 1010 (window now late)\");\n        harness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1010L));\n\n        System.out.println(\"Step 3: Process late element at ts=1003 (should be sent to side output)\");\n        harness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1003L));\n\n        // Check outputs\n        var mainOutput = harness.getOutput();\n        var sideOutput = harness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        System.out.println(\"Main output: \" + mainOutput);\n        System.out.println(\"Side output: \" + sideOutput);\n\n        // The late element should be in the side output\n        assert !sideOutput.isEmpty() : \"Late element should be in side output\";\n\n        harness.close();\n    }\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/TestSessionWindowLateData.java"
}

{
  "content": "import java.time.Duration;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.BasicTypeInfo;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeInformation;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple3;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.EventTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.EventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.TimeWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.WindowOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.WindowOperatorTestBase;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.watermark.Watermark;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n\n/**\n * Test to demonstrate late data side output handling for merging windows.\n *\n * This test exercises the scenario where:\n * 1. An element arrives late (after watermark)\n * 2. Could potentially merge with an existing non-expired window\n * 3. Should be emitted to side output\n */\npublic class TestSessionWindowLateData {\n\n    private final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late\"){};\n\n    @Test\n    void testLateElementInMergingSessionWindow() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 5;  // 5 second gap\n        final long allowedLateness = 0;  // No allowed lateness\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        (a, b) -> new Tuple2<>(a.f0, a.f1 + b.f1),\n                        TypeInformation.of(new org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils.GenericTypeInfo<>(\n                                Tuple2.class,\n                                String.class,\n                                Integer.class\n                        )));\n\n        WindowOperator<String, Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, TimeWindow> operator =\n                new WindowOperator<>(\n                        EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                        new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                        new org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public String getKey(Tuple2<String, Integer> value) {\n                                return value.f0;\n                            }\n                        },\n                        BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                new org.apache.flink.api.common.ExecutionConfig().getSerializerConfig()),\n                        stateDesc,\n                        new InternalWindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                                String, TimeWindow>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public void process(String key, TimeWindow window,\n                                    InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext context,\n                                    Tuple2<String, Integer> input,\n                                    org.apache.flink.util.Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out) {\n                                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n                            }\n\n                            @Override\n                            public void clear(TimeWindow window,\n                                    InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext context) {\n                            }\n                        },\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        allowedLateness,\n                        lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                harness = new OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<>(operator);\n\n        harness.open();\n\n        // Scenario:\n        // 1. Element arrives at timestamp 1000, creates window [1000, 1005]\n        // 2. Watermark advances to 1010 (window is now \"late\" - cleanup time is 1005)\n        // 3. Another element arrives at timestamp 1003 (which is LATE - 1003 + 0 <= 1010)\n        // 4. This element COULD merge with the first window, creating [1000, 1008]\n        // 5. But the merged window is ALSO late (1008 + 0 <= 1010)\n        // 6. The element should be sent to side output, but might be silently dropped\n\n        System.out.println(\"Step 1: Process element at ts=1000\");\n        harness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1000L));\n\n        System.out.println(\"Step 2: Advance watermark to 1010 (window now late)\");\n        harness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1010L));\n\n        System.out.println(\"Step 3: Process late element at ts=1003 (should be sent to side output)\");\n        harness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1003L));\n\n        // Check outputs\n        var mainOutput = harness.getOutput();\n        var sideOutput = harness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        System.out.println(\"Main output: \" + mainOutput);\n        System.out.println(\"Side output: \" + sideOutput);\n\n        // The late element should be in the side output\n        assert !sideOutput.isEmpty() : \"Late element should be in side output\";\n\n        harness.close();\n    }\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/TestSessionWindowLateData.java",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();",
    "filePath": "/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java",
    "numLines": 101,
    "startLine": 303,
    "totalLines": 1017
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java",
  "new_string": "                // drop if the window is already late\n                // BUT: if merging happened (actualWindow != window), don't drop it\n                // because the element is extending an existing non-expired window\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow) && actualWindow.equals(window)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }",
