org.apache.flink.metrics.MetricGroup;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.DefaultKeyedStateStore;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.KeyedStateBackend;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.VoidNamespace;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.VoidNamespaceSerializer;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalAppendingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalMergingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.graph.StreamConfig;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.AbstractUdfStreamOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimer;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimerService;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OneInputStreamOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OperatorAttributes;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OperatorAttributesBuilder;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.Output;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.TimestampedCollector;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.Triggerable;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.GlobalWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.tasks.ProcessingTimeService;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.tasks.StreamTask;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n                .build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n     * triggerContext object.\n     */\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n        processContext.window = window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n     *\n     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n     */\n    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n     */\n    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n     * the given window.\n     */\n    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n     *\n     * @param element The element to check\n     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n     */\n    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n            return;\n        }\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n     */\n    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        } else {\n            return window.maxTimestamp();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n     * state access for {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n\n        // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n        // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n        protected W window;\n\n        public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(\n                    keyedStateBackend,\n                    new SerializerFactory() {\n                        @Override\n                        public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n                                TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n                            return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n                                    executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\n                        }\n                    });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\n     */\n    public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public MergingWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\n                AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Regular per-window state store for use with {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public class PerWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public PerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor)\n                throws Exception {\n            return keyedStateBackend.getPartitionedState(window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A utility class for handling {@code ProcessWindowFunction} invocations. This can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code WindowContext}.\n     */\n    public class WindowContext implements InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext {\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected AbstractPerWindowStateStore windowState;\n\n        public WindowContext(W window) {\n            this.window = window;\n            this.windowState =\n                    windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner\n                            ? new MergingWindowStateStore(\n                                    getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig())\n                            : new PerWindowStateStore(getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"WindowContext{Window = \" + window.toString() + \"}\";\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            userFunction.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore windowState() {\n            this.windowState.window = this.window;\n            return this.windowState;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore globalState() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getKeyedStateStore();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <X> void output(OutputTag<X> outputTag, X value) {\n            if (outputTag == null) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"OutputTag must not be null.\");\n            }\n            output.collect(outputTag, new StreamRecord<>(value, window.maxTimestamp()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code Context} is a utility for handling {@code Trigger} invocations. It can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code Context}\n     */\n    public class Context implements Trigger.OnMergeContext {\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected Collection<W> mergedWindows;\n\n        public Context(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            try {\n                return WindowOperator.this.getPartitionedState(\n                        window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n            } catch (Exception e) {\n                throw new RuntimeException(\"Could not retrieve state\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <S extends MergingState<?, ?>> void mergePartitionedState(\n                StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            if (mergedWindows != null && mergedWindows.size() > 0) {\n                try {\n                    S rawState =\n                            getKeyedStateBackend()\n                                    .getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n\n                    if (rawState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n                        InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?> mergingState =\n                                (InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?>) rawState;\n                        mergingState.mergeNamespaces(window, mergedWindows);\n                    } else {\n                        throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n                                \"The given state descriptor does not refer to a mergeable state (MergingState)\");\n                    }\n                } catch (Exception e) {\n                    throw new RuntimeException(\"Error while merging state.\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onElement(element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onProcessingTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onEventTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void onMerge(Collection<W> mergedWindows) throws Exception {\n            this.mergedWindows = mergedWindows;\n            trigger.onMerge(window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            trigger.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"Context{\" + \"key=\" + key + \", window=\" + window + '}';\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Internal class for keeping track of in-flight timers. */\n    protected static class Timer<K, W extends Window> implements Comparable<Timer<K, W>> {\n        protected long timestamp;\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public Timer(long timestamp, K key, W window) {\n            this.timestamp = timestamp;\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int compareTo(Timer<K, W> o) {\n            return Long.compare(this.timestamp, o.timestamp);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean equals(Object o) {\n            if (this == o) {\n                return true;\n            }\n            if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            Timer<?, ?> timer = (Timer<?, ?>) o;\n\n            return timestamp == timer.timestamp\n                    && key.equals(timer.key)\n                    && window.equals(timer.window);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int hashCode() {\n            int result = (int) (timestamp ^ (timestamp >>> 32));\n            result = 31 * result + key.hashCode();\n            result = 31 * result + window.hashCode();\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"Timer{\"\n                    + \"timestamp=\"\n                    + timestamp\n                    + \", key=\"\n                    + key\n                    + \", window=\"\n                    + window\n                    + '}';\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> getTrigger() {\n        return trigger;\n    }\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public KeySelector<IN, K> getKeySelector() {\n        return keySelector;\n    }\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> getWindowAssigner() {\n        return windowAssigner;\n    }\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n        return windowStateDescriptor;\n    }\n}\n",

        "                 // drop if the window is already late",
        "-                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {",
        "+                // BUT: if merging happened (actualWindow != window), don't drop it",

        "+                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow) && actualWindow.equals(window)) {",
        "                     mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);",

is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.annotation.Internal;\nimport org.apache.flink.annotation.VisibleForTesting;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AppendingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.StateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils.TypeSerializer;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector;\nimport org.apache.flink.metrics.MetricGroup;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimer;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.evictors.Evictor;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.base.Function;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.FluentIterable;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * A {@link WindowOperator} that also allows an {@link Evictor} to be used.\n *\n * <p>The {@code Evictor} is used to remove elements from a pane before/after the evaluation of\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} and after the window evaluation gets triggered by a {@link\n * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger}.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class EvictingWindowOperator<K, IN, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends WindowOperator<K, IN, Iterable<IN>, OUT, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // these fields are set by the API stream graph builder to configure the operator\n\n    private final Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?>\n            evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // the fields below are instantiated once the operator runs in the runtime\n\n    private transient EvictorContext evictorContext;\n\n    private transient InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>> evictingWindowState;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    public EvictingWindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends ListState<StreamRecord<IN>>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<Iterable<IN>, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> evictor,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(\n                windowAssigner,\n                windowSerializer,\n                keySelector,\n                keySerializer,\n                null,\n                windowFunction,\n                trigger,\n                allowedLateness,\n                lateDataOutputTag);\n\n        this.evictor = checkNotNull(evictor);\n        this.evictingWindowStateDescriptor = checkNotNull(windowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentProcessingTime()) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                            + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentProcessingTime()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        evictingWindowState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // check if the window is already inactive\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                evictingWindowState.add(element);\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n                evictorContext.key = key;\n                evictorContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n                    if (contents == null) {\n                        // if we have no state, there is nothing to do\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    emitWindowContents(window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    evictingWindowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n        evictorContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        evictorContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = null;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            evictingWindowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents = evictingWindowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents, evictingWindowState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            evictingWindowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, evictingWindowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void emitWindowContents(\n            W window, Iterable<StreamRecord<IN>> contents, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState)\n            throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // Work around type system restrictions...\n        FluentIterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> recordsWithTimestamp =\n                FluentIterable.from(contents)\n                        .transform(\n                                new Function<StreamRecord<IN>, TimestampedValue<IN>>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public TimestampedValue<IN> apply(StreamRecord<IN> input) {\n                                        return TimestampedValue.from(input);\n                                    }\n                                });\n        evictorContext.evictBefore(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        FluentIterable<IN> projectedContents =\n                recordsWithTimestamp.transform(\n                        new Function<TimestampedValue<IN>, IN>() {\n                            @Override\n                            public IN apply(TimestampedValue<IN> input) {\n                                return input.getValue();\n                            }\n                        });\n\n        processContext.window = triggerContext.window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key,\n                triggerContext.window,\n                processContext,\n                projectedContents,\n                timestampedCollector);\n        evictorContext.evictAfter(recordsWithTimestamp, Iterables.size(recordsWithTimestamp));\n\n        // work around to fix FLINK-4369, remove the evicted elements from the windowState.\n        // this is inefficient, but there is no other way to remove elements from ListState, which\n        // is an AppendingState.\n        windowState.update(\n                recordsWithTimestamp.stream()\n                        .map(TimestampedValue::getStreamRecord)\n                        .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n    }\n\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, ListState<StreamRecord<IN>> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code EvictorContext} is a utility for handling {@code Evictor} invocations. It can be\n     * reused by setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept\n     * in the {@code EvictorContext}.\n     */\n    class EvictorContext implements Evictor.EvictorContext {\n\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public EvictorContext(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return EvictingWindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public K getKey() {\n            return key;\n        }\n\n        void evictBefore(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictBefore((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n\n        void evictAfter(Iterable<TimestampedValue<IN>> elements, int size) {\n            evictor.evictAfter((Iterable) elements, size, window, this);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        evictorContext = new EvictorContext(null, null);\n        evictingWindowState =\n                (InternalListState<K, W, StreamRecord<IN>>)\n                        getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, evictingWindowStateDescriptor);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        evictorContext = null;\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Getters for testing\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    public Evictor<? super IN, ? super W> getEvictor() {\n        return evictor;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    @VisibleForTesting\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked, rawtypes\")\n    public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\n        return (StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, Iterable<IN>>, ?>)\n                evictingWindowStateDescriptor;\n    }\n}\n",

  "file": {
    "content": "                // drop if the window is already late\n                // BUT: if merging happened (actualWindow != window), don't drop it\n                // because the element is extending an existing non-expired window\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow) && actualWindow.equals(window)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);",

  "file": {
    "content": "    @Test\n    void testNotSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithHugeLateness() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // the maxTimestamp of the merged session is already late,\n        // so we get an immediate firing\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-7\", 1000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-8\", 1000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n",

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
  "new_string": "    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testLateElementWithMergingWindowsNotSilentlyDropped() throws Exception {\n        // Test for the bug where a late element that could extend an existing session window\n        // was being silently dropped instead of being processed or sent to side output.\n        // The bug occurred because we checked isWindowLate() without considering whether\n        // a merge with an existing window had occurred.\n\n        final int gapSize = 5; // 5 second gap\n        final long allowedLateness = 0; // No allowed lateness\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Step 1: Process first element at timestamp 1000\n        // This creates window [1000, 1005]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1001));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1001));\n\n        // Step 2: Process another element at timestamp 1002\n        // This extends the window to [1000, 1007]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1002));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1003));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1003));\n\n        // Step 3: Advance watermark to 1010\n        // Now the window [1000, 1007] is considered \"late\" for cleanup\n        // (cleanup time = 1007 + 0 = 1007, and 1007 < 1010)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1010));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1010));\n\n        // Step 4: Process a late element at timestamp 1004\n        // This SHOULD merge with the existing window [1000, 1007] to create [1000, 1009]\n        // Even though the resulting merged window [1000, 1009] would be \"late\"\n        // (cleanup time = 1009, and 1009 < 1010 is false, so it's NOT late!)\n        // The element should be processed because it extends the existing window.\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1004));\n\n        // The window should fire with all elements [1, 1, 1] = 3\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key-3\", 1000L, 1009L), 1008));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)",
  "old_string": "    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testLateElementWithMergingWindowsNotSilentlyDropped() throws Exception {\n        // Test for the bug where a late element that could extend an existing session window\n        // was being silently dropped instead of being processed or sent to side output.\n        // The bug occurred because we checked isWindowLate() without considering whether\n        // a merge with an existing window had occurred.\n\n        final int gapSize = 5; // 5 second gap\n        final long allowedLateness = 0; // No allowed lateness\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Step 1: Process first element at timestamp 1000\n        // This creates window [1000, 1005]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1001));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1001));\n\n        // Step 2: Process another element at timestamp 1002\n        // This extends the window to [1000, 1007]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1002));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1003));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1003));\n\n        // Step 3: Advance watermark to 1010\n        // Now the window [1000, 1007] is considered \"late\" for cleanup\n        // (cleanup time = 1007 + 0 = 1007, and 1007 < 1010)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1010));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1010));\n\n        // Step 4: Process a late element at timestamp 1004\n        // This SHOULD merge with the existing window [1000, 1007] to create [1000, 1009]\n        // Even though the resulting merged window [1000, 1009] would be \"late\"\n        // (cleanup time = 1009, and 1009 < 1010 is false, so it's NOT late!)\n        // The element should be processed because it extends the existing window.\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key\", 1), 1004));\n\n        // The window should fire with all elements [1, 1, 1] = 3\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key-3\", 1000L, 1009L), 1008));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)",

org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.base.Joiner;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport
